full_name: Cussen Lunar Prospection Index — Water variant

components.name: WEH concentration
components.def: norm(H ppm × 9) from LP-NS Feldman 2001 hydrogen map

components.name: Polar latitude factor
components.def: |lat|/90 — PSR access bonus, opposite of CLPI-ART C5

components.name: Cold-trap density bonus
components.def: Gaussian bonus around |lat|=90 (σ=12°) — soft proxy for PSR cluster density without explicit LOLA illumination model

data_sources: LP-NS hydrogen map (Feldman et al. 2001, JGR 106, 23231) — historical standard
LOLA LDEM 16 ppd (Smith et al. 2010) — topography + roughness

lcross_calibration.note: LCROSS measured localized ejecta from a specific PSR; Feldman 2001 is a bulk 2°×2° average including non-PSR terrain. The ~100× ratio between the LCROSS plume and the Feldman bin average is consistent with the PSR hosting a concentrated cold-trap hotspot within an otherwise moderate bin.

nasa_artemis3_comparison.interpretation: CLPI-H2O is the correct index for Artemis III-class missions. It scores the NASA polar candidate sites significantly higher than CLPI-ART because it optimizes the resource they actually target (water). This closes the loop: CLPI-ART + CLPI-HE3 + CLPI-H2O cover the three main lunar ISRU resource classes (ilmenite, helium-3, water).

limitations: LP-NS is a 2001-vintage product. LRO/LEND (2010) gives higher spatial resolution at the south pole but is not openly curl-able.
Feldman 2001 WEH is a bulk 2° average. True PSR hotspots are sub-km features averaged out by the neutron footprint (~60 km).
W4 (cold-trap bonus) is a soft Gaussian proxy, not a real LOLA illumination model. A rigorous PSR map requires ray-tracing LOLA 5m DEM (Mazarico et al. 2011 method).
Feldman 2001 hydrogen does NOT distinguish water ice from hydroxyl, sulfates, or solar-wind implanted H. LCROSS 2009, SOFIA 2020, Chang'e-5 2022 confirmed H2O molecular form at specific sites.
The conversion H × 9 assumes ALL hydrogen is in H2O molecular form. Real mixture includes hydroxyl and bound H in silicates; WEH is an upper bound on actual water.
references_water: Feldman et al. 2001, JGR 106 — LP-NS hydrogen map
Colaprete et al. 2010, Science 330 — LCROSS Cabeus detection
Pieters et al. 2009, Science 326 — Chandrayaan-1 M³ OH/H2O
Mitrofanov et al. 2010, Science 330 — LRO/LEND hydrogen mapping
Spudis et al. 2013, JGR 118 — LRO Mini-RF polar ice
Honniball et al. 2020, Nature Astronomy 5 — SOFIA molecular water
Hu et al. 2022, Nature 600 — Chang'e-5 sample water 120 ppm
